generatedSentence: Elara, a dreamer with a perpetually curious mind, found herself haunted by a persistent headache. Her backpack, decked out with vogue-era patches, felt heavier than a kilo of lead. She often visited a dark website, sifting through heretical theories about cyborgs and ancient werewolf lore, seeking a valid syntax for the chaotic world. The gimmick of perceived entitlement and nonjob fortunes, like a jackpot win, felt incredibly unfair to her sense of fairness. She yearned for harmony, a variety of perspectives beyond the acidic squawking of parrots. A bluejay took liftoff outside her window, a momentary drippy of grace. One instance, while on the expressway, she spotted a graphic distortion, causing her eyesight to blur, almost leading to catfights with an evasive skunk. She usually wore a garment that made her looky, but today she felt like a whipped whippet, her nerves frayed. Below the elegant posture of society, a gamecock mentality persisted, extremely reckless in its pursuit of economy, ready to impeach any dissenting gentleman. Frankly, the idea of these tycoons, resembling mockingbird parrots, creating a landmine of financial insecurity, made her stomach jellify, a nasty stink rising from the system's underbelly. The device, a neural jammer, intended to incapacitate any who would resist, was their latest payload. The miners, resembling Kalmuck warriors, beneath the shimmering evergreen canopy, faced the raunchy reality of a payback scheme. She watched a meteor streaking across the savannah, a tearful reunion of light and dark, as if the occident itself wept. It was a vital objective, not merely a gimmick, to remove this device, a task that required heroes to dazzle and ducking through rapids. The ventilation hummed, a reminder of the machine's relentless name. Suddenly, a narwhal-like creature emerged from beneath the currents, its powerful winger balancing the odds, its presence a familiar keyword for hope. It was a wonderful instance of natural utility, its gentle kissy snout nudging a dropped device. Baffled, the forces of entitlement, now without their jammer, could only watch their mahjong game of power unravel, their rollers stilled. The land’s tillage could finally dignify itself, free from the nonjob exploiters. Elara knew then that justice, though sometimes slow, had an undeniable nerve, securing victory for fairness and the grasses of the nestling world.
panelSentences: Elara, a dreamer with a perpetually curious mind, found herself haunted by a persistent headache. Her backpack, decked out with vogue-era patches, felt heavier than a kilo of lead. She often visited a dark website, sifting through heretical theories about cyborgs and ancient werewolf lore, seeking a valid syntax for the chaotic world. The gimmick of perceived entitlement and nonjob fortunes, like a jackpot win, felt incredibly unfair to her sense of fairness. She yearned for harmony, a variety of perspectives beyond the acidic squawking of parrots. A bluejay took liftoff outside her window, a momentary drippy of grace.

One instance, while on the expressway, she spotted a graphic distortion, causing her eyesight to blur, almost leading to catfights with an evasive skunk. She usually wore a garment that made her looky, but today she felt like a whipped whippet, her nerves frayed. Below the elegant posture of society, a gamecock mentality persisted, extremely reckless in its pursuit of economy, ready to impeach any dissenting gentleman. Frankly, the idea of these tycoons, resembling mockingbird parrots, creating a landmine of financial insecurity, made her stomach jellify, a nasty stink rising from the system's underbelly.

The device, a neural jammer, intended to incapacitate any who would resist, was their latest payload. The miners, resembling Kalmuck warriors, beneath the shimmering evergreen canopy, faced the raunchy reality of a payback scheme. She watched a meteor streaking across the savannah, a tearful reunion of light and dark, as if the occident itself wept. It was a vital objective, not merely a gimmick, to remove this device, a task that required heroes to dazzle and ducking through rapids. The ventilation hummed, a reminder of the machine's relentless name.

Suddenly, a narwhal-like creature emerged from beneath the currents, its powerful winger balancing the odds, its presence a familiar keyword for hope. It was a wonderful instance of natural utility, its gentle kissy snout nudging a dropped device. Baffled, the forces of entitlement, now without their jammer, could only watch their mahjong game of power unravel, their rollers stilled. The land’s tillage could finally dignify itself, free from the nonjob exploiters. Elara knew then that justice, though sometimes slow, had an undeniable nerve, securing victory for fairness and the grasses of the nestling world.
rawPanelSentences: Elara, a dreamer with a perpetually curious mind, found herself haunted by a persistent headache. Her backpack, decked out with vogue-era patches, felt heavier than a kilo of lead. She often visited a dark website, sifting through heretical theories about cyborgs and ancient werewolf lore, seeking a valid syntax for the chaotic world. The gimmick of perceived entitlement and nonjob fortunes, like a jackpot win, felt incredibly unfair to her sense of fairness. She yearned for harmony, a variety of perspectives beyond the acidic squawking of parrots. A bluejay took liftoff outside her window, a momentary drippy of grace.

One instance, while on the expressway, she spotted a graphic distortion, causing her eyesight to blur, almost leading to catfights with an evasive skunk. She usually wore a garment that made her looky, but today she felt like a whipped whippet, her nerves frayed. Below the elegant posture of society, a gamecock mentality persisted, extremely reckless in its pursuit of economy, ready to impeach any dissenting gentleman. Frankly, the idea of these tycoons, resembling mockingbird parrots, creating a landmine of financial insecurity, made her stomach jellify, a nasty stink rising from the system's underbelly.

The device, a neural jammer, intended to incapacitate any who would resist, was their latest payload. The miners, resembling Kalmuck warriors, beneath the shimmering evergreen canopy, faced the raunchy reality of a payback scheme. She watched a meteor streaking across the savannah, a tearful reunion of light and dark, as if the occident itself wept. It was a vital objective, not merely a gimmick, to remove this device, a task that required heroes to dazzle and ducking through rapids. The ventilation hummed, a reminder of the machine's relentless name.

Suddenly, a narwhal-like creature emerged from beneath the currents, its powerful winger balancing the odds, its presence a familiar keyword for hope. It was a wonderful instance of natural utility, its gentle kissy snout nudging a dropped device. Baffled, the forces of entitlement, now without their jammer, could only watch their mahjong game of power unravel, their rollers stilled. The land’s tillage could finally dignify itself, free from the nonjob exploiters. Elara knew then that justice, though sometimes slow, had an undeniable nerve, securing victory for fairness and the grasses of the nestling world.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A young woman with a cybernetic eye patch, wearing a sleek, form-fitting jumpsuit of deep blue and silver, carrying a high-tech backpack. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, illuminated by a glowing data stream from a holographic website, sitting at a desk with an expression of intense curiosity, viewed from a medium shot, slightly high angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist Elara's face, close-up, eyes wide with a mix of shock and determination, as an abstract 'stink' graphic emanates from a distant, blurred cityscape, viewed from a low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, now dressed in hero attire, leaping over glowing rapids within a cavernous, high-tech mine, her silhouette dramatically lit against an approaching meteor, viewed from a dynamic action shot, eye-level. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist Elara, standing triumphant on a grassy savannah, alongside a majestic, robotic narwhal-like creature, looking towards a receding, defeated cityscape, viewed from a wide, triumphant shot, slightly low angle.

definitions.definition: Well grounded or justifiable, pertinent.
definitions.example: I will believe him as soon as he offers a valid answer.

definitions.definition: Acceptable, proper or correct; in accordance with the rules.
definitions.example: A valid format for the date is MM/DD/YY.

definitions.definition: Related to the current topic, or presented within context, relevant.
Of a formula or system: such that it evaluates to true regardless of the input values.
Of an argument: whose conclusion is always true whenever its premises are true.
definitions.example: An argument is valid if and only if the set consisting of both (1) all of its premises and (2) the contradictory of its conclusion is inconsistent.

definitions.definition: Effective.
definitions.example: He is a priest now: although his ordination was contrary to the law of the church, it was still valid.

definitions.definition: A species of North American jay with blue feathering from the top of its head to midway down its back, Cyanocitta cristata.
One of the blue-marked species of the Old World bird genus Coracias.

A knapsack, sometimes mounted on a light frame, but always supported by straps, worn on a person’s back for the purpose of carrying things, especially when hiking, or on a student's back when carrying books.
A similarly placed item containing a parachute or other life-support equipment.
To hike and camp overnight in backcountry with one's gear carried in a backpack
To engage in low-cost, generally urban, travel with minimal luggage and frugal accommodations
To place or carry (an item or items) in a backpack
To furnish with a deck, as a vessel.
To knock someone to the floor, especially with a single punch.
definitions.example: Wow, did you see her deck that guy who pinched her?

definitions.definition: To cause a player to run out of cards to draw and usually lose the game as a result.
(sometimes with out) To dress (someone) up, to clothe with more than ordinary elegance
(sometimes with out) To decorate (something).
To cover; to overspread.
Adorned or embellished.
(in combination) Having a specified number or type of decks.
Dripping or tending to drip.
Rainy or wet.
Maudlin, tiresome or annoying;
A person who is part machine, a robot who is part organic.
A robot who has an organic past.
A human with electronic or bionic prostheses.
Tending to ask questions, or to want to explore or investigate; inquisitive; (with a negative connotation) nosy, prying.
definitions.example: Young children are naturally curious about the world and everything in it.

definitions.definition: Caused by curiosity.
Leading one to ask questions about; somewhat odd, out of the ordinary, or unusual.
definitions.example: The platypus is a curious creature, with fur like a mammal and a beak like a bird.

definitions.definition: Careful, fastidious, particular; (specifically) demanding a high standard of excellence, difficult to satisfy.
Carefully or artfully constructed; made with great elegance or skill.
Having a pH less than 7, or being sour, or having the strength to neutralize alkalis, or turning a litmus paper red.
Containing a high percentage of silica; opposed to basic.
Of or relating to acid; having the character of an acid.
A person who is transformed or can transform into a wolf or a wolflike human, often said to transform during a full moon.
A collection of interlinked web pages on the World Wide Web that are typically accessible from the same base URL and reside on the same server.
A set of rules that govern how words are combined to form phrases and sentences.
The formal rules of formulating the statements of a computer language.
The study of the structure of phrases, sentences and language.

In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
A trick or device used to attain some end.
definitions.example: The box had a gimmick to make the coin appear to vanish.

definitions.definition: A clever ploy or strategy.
definitions.example: The contest was a gimmick to get people to sign up for their mailing list.

definitions.definition: A gimmick capacitor.
A night out with one's friends.
To rig or set up with a trick or device.
definitions.example: The magician's box was gimmicked with a wire that made it appear to open on its own.

definitions.definition: A kind of bird, many species of which are colourful and able to mimic human speech, of the order Psittaciformes or (narrowly) of the family Psittacidae.
definitions.example: I bought a wonderful parrot at the pet store.

definitions.definition: A parroter; a person who repeats the words or ideas of others.
definitions.example: What kind of a parrot are you? He just said that.

definitions.definition: A puffin.
Channel coal.
A transponder.
To repeat (exactly what has just been said) without necessarily showing understanding, in the manner of a parrot.
definitions.example: The interviewee merely parroted the views of her tabloid.

definitions.definition: The right to have something, whether actual or perceived.
Power, authority to do something.
Something that one is entitled to.
A legal obligation on a government to make payments to a person, business, or unit of government that meets the criteria set in law, such as social security in the US.
A position that is ostensibly a job but does not involve useful or meaningful work.
The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female mammals, milk is secreted.
A mechanical device through which liquids or gases can be passed in a regulated manner.
An artificial nipple (definition 1) used for bottle-feeding infants.
Any small physical protrusion, such as the lumps on the F and J keys on computer keyboards.
Any small physical protrusion on an automotive, a machine part or any other part that fits into a groove on another part.
A perforated segment that fits into part of the breech of a muzzle-loading gun, on which the percussion cap is fixed.
A short tube threaded at both ends, used as a connector.
An internally threaded piece which holds a bicycle spoke in place on the rim.
definitions.example: Turn the nipples on to the bicycle spokes only one turn, for each spoke on the new wheel all the way around until they are all snug, then check alignment.

definitions.definition: The point in the launch of a rocket or an aircraft where it leaves contact with the ground.
The point at which a person or animal leaves the ground, as for example when jumping.
The property of being fair or equitable.
definitions.example: Some questioned the fairness of the new laws.

definitions.definition: The property of being fair or beautiful.
A money prize pool which accumulates until the conditions are met for it to be won.
A large cash prize or money.
An unexpected windfall or reward.
A concentration of surface wood or fuel.
definitions.example: a juniper jackpot

definitions.definition: A large accumulated point bonus, originally awarded after a long set of actions but now often easily available in multiball modes.
(of a slot machine) To issue a jackpot.
The prevailing fashion or style.
definitions.example: Miniskirts were the vogue in the '60s.

definitions.definition: Popularity or a current craze.
definitions.example: Hula hoops are no longer in vogue.

definitions.definition: A highly stylized modern dance that evolved out of the Harlem ballroom scene in the 1960s.
A cigarette.
To dance in the vogue dance style.
To light a cigarette.
definitions.example: Vogue me up.

definitions.definition: The quality of being varied; diversity.
definitions.example: Variety is the spice of life.

definitions.definition: A specific variation of something.
A number of different things.
A state of constant change.
A rank in a taxonomic classification, below species and subspecies.
The total number of distinct states of a system.
Logarithm of the base 2 of the total number of distinct states of a system.
A term used for a specific form of a language, neutral to whether that form is a dialect, accent, register, etc. and to its prestige level.
(universal algebra) An equational class; the class of all algebraic structures of a given signature, satisfying a given set of identities.
An algebraic variety.
The kind of theatrical entertainment given in variety shows.
The production of, or performance in, variety shows.
One who dreams.
Someone whose beliefs are far from realistic.
Any anglerfish of the family Oneirodidae.
Swallow-wing puffbird (Chelidoptera tenebrosa)
A pain or ache in the head.
A nuisance or unpleasant problem.
definitions.example: The clumsy filing system has been a huge headache.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to heresy or heretics.
(of ideas or views) Contrary to mainstream or accepted opinion.
Agreement or accord.
A pleasing combination of elements, or arrangement of sounds.
The academic study of chords.
Two or more notes played simultaneously to produce a chord.
The relationship between two distinct musical pitches (musical pitches being frequencies of vibration which produce audible sound) played simultaneously.
A literary work which brings together or arranges systematically parallel passages of historians respecting the same events, and shows their agreement or consistency.
definitions.example: a harmony of the Gospels

definitions.definition: A strong bad smell.
A complaint or objection.
definitions.example: If you don't make a stink about the problem, nothing will be done.

definitions.definition: To have a strong bad smell.
To be greatly inferior; to perform badly.
definitions.example: That movie stinks. I didn't even stay for the end.

definitions.definition: To give an impression of dishonesty or untruth.
definitions.example: Something stinks about the politician's excuses.

definitions.definition: To cause to stink; to affect by a stink.
Bad; inferior; worthless.
definitions.example: The concert was stink. / That was a stink concert.

definitions.definition: Bad-smelling, stinky.

Any of various small mammals, of the family Mephitidae, native to North and Central America, having a glossy black with a white coat and two musk glands at the base of the tail for emitting a noxious smell as a defensive measure.
A despicable person.
A walkover victory in sports or board games, as when the opposing side is unable to score. Compare shutout.
A win by 30 or more points.
To defeat so badly as to prevent any opposing points.
definitions.example: I skunked him at cards.

definitions.definition: To win by 30 or more points.
(of beer) To go bad, to spoil.
A fighting cock: a rooster used in cockfighting.
(degree) To an extreme degree.
A wealthy and powerful business person.
definitions.example: Chairman Yu is a tycoon who owns multiple hotel chains.

definitions.definition: A long-tailed American songbird of the Mimidae family, noted for its ability to mimic calls of other birds.
(sometimes humorous) Look.
(manner) In a frank, open or (too) honest manner.
definitions.example: He spoke frankly about the economy.

definitions.definition: (sentence adverb) In truth, to tell the truth.
A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
A receptacle for oil in a lamp.
Spring, source, fountain.

A set of glyphs of unified design, belonging to one typeface (e.g., Helvetica), style (e.g., italic), and weight (e.g., bold). Usually representing the letters of an alphabet and its supplementary characters.
A computer file containing the code used to draw and compose the glyphs of one or more typographic fonts on a computer display or printer.
To overlay (text) on the picture.
A source, wellspring, fount.
Careless or heedless; headstrong or rash.
Indifferent to danger or the consequences.
To form a jelly; to gel.
To make into a jelly.
The quality of being decent; propriety.
That which is proper or becoming.
A material object that physically exists.
A goal that is striven for.
(grammar) The objective case.
(grammar) a noun or pronoun in the objective case.
The lens or lenses of a camera, microscope, or other optical device closest to the object being examined.
The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.
Something that is useful.
The ability of a commodity to satisfy needs or wants; the satisfaction experienced by the consumer of that commodity.
Well-being, satisfaction, pleasure, or happiness.
A service provider, such as an electric company or water company; or, the securities of such a provider.
A software program designed to perform a single task or a small range of tasks, often to help manage and tune computer hardware, an operating system or application software.
definitions.example: I've bought a new disk utility that can recover deleted files.

definitions.definition: The ability to play multiple positions.
Having to do with, or owned by, a service provider.
definitions.example: utility line; utility bill

definitions.definition: Designating of a room in a house or building where mechanical equipment is installed; such as a furnace, water tank/heater, circuit breaker, and/or air conditioning unit; and often equipped with hookups for laundry equipment (washer/dryer).
definitions.example: utility room

definitions.definition: (interrogative) By what, in which direction; how.
definitions.example: Whereby goest thou?

definitions.definition: By which.
Where, wherein, in which.
Vision or the faculty of sight.
View or the range of vision.
A divided highway where intersections and direct access to adjacent properties have been eliminated.
(parts of the US) A road built to freeway standards.
A road built for high speed traffic, but not up to motorway standards or designated a motorway.
Tending to avoid speaking openly or making revelations about oneself.
Directed towards avoidance or escape; evasive action.
A small horse; a pony.
An old useless horse.
A paramour.
The cultivation of arable land by plowing, sowing and raising crops.
Land cultivated in this way.
A mine that is placed on land and designed to explode when stepped upon or touched.
To sow (an area) with land mines.
An attendant spirit, often in animal or demon form.
definitions.example: The witch’s familiar was a black cat.

definitions.definition: A member of one's family or household.
A member of a pope's or bishop's household.
A close friend.
The officer of the Inquisition who arrested suspected people.
Known to one, or generally known; commonplace.
definitions.example: there’s a familiar face; that tune sounds familiar

definitions.definition: Acquainted.
definitions.example: I'm quite familiar with this system; she's not familiar with manual gears

definitions.definition: Intimate or friendly.
definitions.example: we are not on familiar terms; our neighbour is not familiar

definitions.definition: Inappropriately intimate or friendly.
definitions.example: Don’t be familiar with me, boy!

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to a family; familial.
Relating to, or characteristic of life.
definitions.example: vital energies; vital functions; vital actions

definitions.definition: Necessary to the continuation of life; being the seat of life; being that on which life depends.
definitions.example: The brain is a vital organ.

definitions.definition: Invigorating or life-giving.
Necessary to continued existence.
definitions.example: The transition to farming was vital for the creation of civilisation.

definitions.definition: Relating to the recording of life events.
definitions.example: Birth, marriage and death certificates are vital records.

definitions.definition: Very important.
definitions.example: It is vital that you don't forget to do your homework.

definitions.definition: Containing life; living.
Capable of living; in a state to live; viable.
To quickly lower the head or body in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To quickly lower (the head) in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To lower (something) into water; to thrust or plunge under liquid and suddenly withdraw.
To go under the surface of water and immediately reappear; to plunge one's head into water or other liquid.
To bow.
To evade doing something.
To lower the volume of (a sound) so that other sounds in the mix can be heard more clearly.
To enter a place for a short moment.
definitions.example: I'm just going to duck into the loo for a minute, can you hold my bag?

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to duck.
An instance of ducking (a person in water, etc).
definitions.example: They gave him a ducking in the river as a punishment.

definitions.definition: A fight between cats.
definitions.example: The caterwauling from the catfight in the back yard was awful; I couldn't get to sleep until it was over.

definitions.definition: An acrimonious fighting or bickering between women; so named because, like cats, scratching is a common defensive tactic among women, as opposed to a fistfight between men.
definitions.example: Nancy and Sheila got into a catfight when Nancy's boyfriend cheated on her with Sheila.

definitions.definition: The part of the horizon where the sun last appears in the evening; that part of the earth towards the sunset; the west.
The Western world; the part of the world excluding Asia
A touch with the lips, usually to express love or affection, or as a greeting.
An 'X' mark placed at the end of a letter or other type of message.
A type of filled chocolate candy, shaped as if someone had kissed the top. See Hershey's Kisses.
Sentimentally affectionate.
Tending to kiss frequently.
definitions.example: We sat behind an obnoxiously kissy couple in the cinema.

definitions.definition: Suited to kissing; resembling or relating to kissing.
definitions.example: kissy lips

definitions.definition: A single item of clothing.
Short for temple garment.
To clothe in a garment.
A small, young bird that is still confined to the nest.
A nest; a receptacle.
A person who works in a mine.
An operator of ordnance mines and similar explosives.
Any bird of one of four species of Australian endemic honeyeaters in the genus Manorina.
Any bird of one of several species of South American ovenbirds in the genus Geositta.
Software or hardware that mines, or creates new units of cryptocurrency.
(often in the plural) a rough section of a river or stream which is difficult to navigate due to the swift and turbulent motion of the water.
A burst of rapid fire.
The process or act of reuniting.
definitions.example: It was a tearful and heartfelt reunion as the trapped miners finally saw their dear loved ones again.

definitions.definition: A planned event at which members of a dispersed group meet together.
definitions.example: Are you going to the reunion this year?

definitions.definition: The replacement of stale or noxious air with fresh.
The mechanical system used to circulate and replace air.
An exchange of views during a discussion.
The public exposure of an issue or topic.
The bodily process of breathing; the inhalation of air to provide oxygen, and the exhalation of spent air to remove carbon dioxide.
The mechanical system used to assist breathing.
To fit out with the necessary clothing; to clothe, put clothes on (something or someone).
definitions.example: He was dressed in the latest fashions.

definitions.definition: To clothe oneself; to put on clothes.
definitions.example: I rose and dressed before daybreak.  It's very cold out. Dress warm.

definitions.definition: To put on the uniform and equipment necessary to play the game.
definitions.example: Due to a left ankle sprain, Kobe Bryant did not dress for the game against Indiana

definitions.definition: Of a man, to allow the genitals to fall to one side or other within the trousers.
definitions.example: Does sir dress to the right or the left?

definitions.definition: To prepare (food) for cooking, especially by seasoning it.
To prepare oneself; to make ready.
To adorn, ornament.
definitions.example: It was time to dress the windows for Christmas again.

definitions.definition: To ornament (a ship) by hoisting the national colours at the peak and mastheads, and setting the jack forward; when "dressed full", the signal flags and pennants are added.
To prepare (a set) by installing the props, scenery, etc.
To treat (a wound, or wounded person).
To prepare for use; to fit for any use; to render suitable for an intended purpose; to get ready.
definitions.example: to dress leather or cloth;  to dress a garden;  to dress grain, by cleansing it;  in mining and metallurgy, to dress ores, by sorting and separating them

definitions.definition: To prepare the surface of (a material; usually stone or lumber).
To manure (land).
To bolt or sift flour.
(sometimes imperative) To arrange in exact continuity of line, as soldiers; commonly to adjust to a straight line and at proper distance; to align.
definitions.example: Right, dress!

definitions.definition: To break and train for use, as a horse or other animal.
Having been subjected to a preparatory process or treatment; treated, prepared.
Prepared for eating, especially by the addition of specific condiments or dressing.
Wearing clothes; attired (now often with qualifying word).
A light of dazzling brilliancy.
Showy brilliance that may stop a person from thinking clearly.
A herd of zebra.
Dazzle camouflage.
To confuse the sight of by means of excessive brightness.
definitions.example: Dazzled by the headlights of the lorry, the deer stopped in the middle of the street.

definitions.definition: To render incapable of thinking clearly; to overwhelm with showiness or brilliance.
definitions.example: The delegates were dazzled by the originality of his arguments.

definitions.definition: To be overpowered by light; to be confused by excess of brightness.
Somebody who possesses great bravery and carries out extraordinary or noble deeds.
A role model.
The protagonist in a work of fiction.
The current player, especially an hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: villain. Not to be confused with hero call.
definitions.example: Let's discuss how to play if the hero has KK, and there's an ace on board.

definitions.definition: A large sandwich made from meats and cheeses; a hero sandwich.
(food styling) The product chosen from several candidates to be photographed.
The eye-catching top portion of a web page, sometimes including a hero image; the portion above the fold.
A tropical grassland with scattered trees
To invest with dignity or honour.
To give distinction to.
To exalt in rank.
(chiefly in the negative) To treat as worthy or acceptable; to indulge or condone by acknowledging.
definitions.example: I will not dignify that comment with a response.

definitions.definition: Tending to excite wonder; surprising, extraordinary.
Surprisingly excellent; very good or admirable, extremely impressive.
definitions.example: They served a wonderful six-course meal.

definitions.definition: Exceedingly, to a great extent.
Any plant of the family Poaceae, characterized by leaves that arise from nodes in the stem and leaf bases that wrap around the stem, especially those grown as ground cover rather than for grain.
Various plants not in family Poaceae that resemble grasses.
A lawn.
Marijuana.
An informer, police informer; one who betrays a group (of criminals, etc) to the authorities.
definitions.example: What just happened must remain secret. Don't be a 'grass.

definitions.definition: Sharp, closely spaced discontinuities in the trace of a cathode-ray tube, produced by random interference.
Noise on an A-scope or similar type of radar display.
The season of fresh grass; spring or summer.
That which is transitory.
(folk etymology) Asparagus; "sparrowgrass".
The surface of a mine.
To lay out on the grass; to knock down (an opponent etc.).
To act as a grass or informer, to betray; to report on (criminals etc) to the authorities.
To cover with grass or with turf.
To feed with grass.
To expose, as flax, on the grass for bleaching, etc.
To bring to the grass or ground; to land.
An act of revenge.
definitions.example: They beat us last year, so this year's win was payback.

definitions.definition: A benefit, reward, a form of recompense.
A return on investment
A refund, reimbursement
A tree or shrub that does not shed its leaves or needles seasonally.
(specifically) A conifer tree.
A news story that can be published or broadcast at any time.
(pharmaceuticals) To extend the term of a patent beyond the normal legal limit, usually through repeated small modifications.
To set the repayment rate of a loan at or below the interest rate, so low that the principal will never be repaid.
Of plants, especially trees, that do not shed their leaves seasonally.
Continually fresh or self-renewing.
Suitable for transmission at any time; not urgent or time-dependent.
Monodon monoceros, an Arctic cetacean that grows to about 20 feet (6 meters) long, the male having a single horn-like tusk, a twisted, pointed canine tooth that projects forward.
To publicly disgrace, especially of a recreant knight.
To hoodwink or deceive (someone).
To bewilder completely; to confuse or perplex.
definitions.example: I am baffled by the contradictions and omissions in the instructions.

definitions.definition: To foil; to thwart.
To struggle in vain.
definitions.example: A ship baffles with the winds.

definitions.definition: Thoroughly confused, puzzled
Having baffles
Any piece of equipment made for a particular purpose, especially a mechanical or electrical one.
definitions.example: There are a number of household devices in a kitchen such as a dishwasher, a garbage disposal, or an electric can opener.

definitions.definition: A peripheral device; an item of hardware.
A project or scheme, often designed to deceive; a stratagem; an artifice.
An improvised explosive device, home-made bomb
A technique that an author or speaker uses to evoke an emotional response in the audience; a rhetorical device.

A motto, emblem, or other mark used to distinguish the bearer from others. A device differs from a badge or cognizance primarily because as it is a personal distinction, and not a badge borne by members of the same house successively.
Power of devising; invention; contrivance.
An image used in whole or in part as a trademark or service mark.
An image or logo denoting official or proprietary authority or provenience.
A spectacle or show.
Opinion; decision.
An act of distorting.
A result of distorting.
A misrepresentation of the truth.
definitions.example: The story he told was a bit of a distortion.

definitions.definition: Noise or other artifacts caused in the electronic reproduction of sound or music.
definitions.example: This recording sounds awful due to the distortion.

definitions.definition: An effect used in music, most commonly on guitars in rock or metal.
(optics) an aberration that causes magnification to change over the field of view.
Most of the time; less than always, but more than occasionally.
definitions.example: Except for one or two days a year, he usually walks to work.

definitions.definition: Under normal conditions.
A kind of shaggy cloth resembling bearskin.
A drawing or picture.
(mostly in plural) A computer-generated image as viewed on a screen forming part of a game or a film etc.
definitions.example: I've just played this new computer game: the graphics are amazing.

definitions.definition: Drawn, pictorial.
Vivid, descriptive, often in relation to depictions of sex or violence.
Having a texture that resembles writing, commonly created by exsolution, devitrification and immiscibility processes in igneous rocks.
definitions.example: graphic granite

definitions.definition: Urgency of manner or words; an urgent request; insistence.
A token; a sign; a symptom or indication.
definitions.example: It sends some precious instance of itself/ After the thing it loves. Hamlet IV. v. ca. 1602

definitions.definition: That which is urgent; motive.
Occasion; order of occurrence.
A case offered as an exemplification or a precedent; an illustrative example.
One of a series of recurring occasions, cases, essentially the same.
A piece of evidence; a proof or sign (of something).
A specific occurrence of something that is created or instantiated, such as a database, or an object of a class in object-oriented programming.

(massively multiplayer online games) A dungeon or other area that is duplicated for each player, or each party of players, that enters it, so that each player or party has a private copy of the area, isolated from other players.
(massively multiplayer online games) An individual copy of such a dungeon or other area.
To mention as a case or example; to refer to; to cite
To cite an example as proof; to exemplify.
A fast-moving streak of light in the night sky caused by the entry of extraterrestrial matter into the earth's atmosphere: A shooting star or falling star.

Any atmospheric phenomenon. (Thus the derivation of meteorology.) These were sometimes classified as aerial or airy meteors (winds), aqueous or watery meteors (hydrometeors: clouds, rain, snow, hail, dew, frost), luminous meteors (rainbows and aurora), and igneous or fiery meteors (lightning and shooting stars).
A prop similar to poi balls, in that it is twirled at the end of a cord or cable.
A striking weapon resembling a track and field hammer consisting of a weight swung at the end of a cable or chain.
Any short-lived source of wonderment.
To move at great speed.
Smutty; indecent.
Lecherous.
Sexually seductive.
Very low-class; inferior; inadequate.
With dishonorable, base and vulgar expression.
The process of moving, or the fact of being removed.
The relocation of a business etc.
The dismissal of someone from office.

An evening funeral ritual in which the coffin holding the deceased is brought, usually from a funeral home, to the church where the funeral mass will be celebrated the following day. Prayers are said before and after the journey, after which mourners are typically received at the home of the deceased.
Murder.
Any device used to jam radio reception.
A form of swimwear used by athletes.
A musician who jams.
A device (e.g. a jumar) which will slide along a rope in one direction but not the other, used in rock-climbing, caving etc.
A player who attempts to score points by making their way past other players.
A male duck.
A dog of a certain breed, similar to a small greyhound, originating in Britain and bred for racing.
To hit with a whip.
definitions.example: The rider whipped the horse.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To hit with any flexible object.
definitions.example: I whipped her with a newspaper.

definitions.definition: To defeat, as in a contest or game.
To mix in a rapid aerating fashion, especially food.
definitions.example: to whip eggs or cream

definitions.definition: To urge into action or obedience.
definitions.example: He whipped the department into shape.

definitions.definition: To enforce a member voting in accordance with party policy.
To bind the end of a rope with twine or other small stuff to prevent its unlaying: fraying or unravelling.
To hoist or purchase by means of a whip.
To sew lightly; specifically, to form (a fabric) into gathers by loosely overcasting the rolled edge and drawing up the thread.
definitions.example: to whip a ruffle

definitions.definition: To throw or kick an object at a high velocity.
definitions.example: He whipped the ball at me.

definitions.definition: To fish a body of water especially by making repeated casts.
To snap back and forth like a whip.
definitions.example: The pennants whipped in the wind.

definitions.definition: To move very fast.
definitions.example: The wind whipped through the valley.

definitions.definition: To move (something) very fast; often with up, out, etc.
To transfer momentum from one skater to another.
To lash with sarcasm, abuse, etc.
To thrash; to beat out, as grain, by striking.
definitions.example: to whip wheat

definitions.definition: Controlled by a spouse or significant other to an unreasonable degree.
definitions.example: He knew he was whipped when he turned down a football game so they could shop for linens.

definitions.definition: Any word used as the key to a code.
Any word used in a reference work to link to other words or other information.
A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.
Any word that occurs in a text more often than normal.
To tag with keywords, as for example to facilitate searching.

A man of gentle but not noble birth, particularly a man of means (originally ownership of property) who does not work for a living but has no official status in a peerage; an armiferous man ranking below a knight.
definitions.example: Being a gentleman, Robert was entitled to shove other commoners into the gongpit but he still had to jump out of the way of the knights to avoid the same fate himself.

definitions.definition: Any well-bred, well-mannered, or charming man.
An effeminate or oversophisticated man.
definitions.example: Well, la-di-da, aren't you just a proper gentleman?

definitions.definition: (polite term of address) Any man.
definitions.example: Please escort this gentleman to the gentlemen's room.

definitions.definition: (usually historical, sometimes derogatory) An amateur or dabbler in any field, particularly those of independent means.
An amateur player, particularly one whose wealth permits him to forego payment.
Effective management of a community or system, or especially its resources.
The study of money, currency and trade, and the efficient use of resources.
Frugal use of resources.
definitions.example: economy of word

definitions.definition: The system of production and distribution and consumption. The overall measure of a currency system; as the national economy.
The method of divine government of the world. (See w:Economy (religion).)
The part of a commercial passenger airplane or train reserved for those paying the lower standard fares; economy class.
Management of one’s residency.
Cheap to run; using minimal resources; representing good value for money.
definitions.example: Economy size.

definitions.definition: Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.
definitions.example: I've never liked the name my parents gave me so I changed it at the age of twenty.

definitions.definition: Reputation.
An abusive or insulting epithet.
definitions.example: Stop calling me names!

definitions.definition: A person (or legal person).
Those of a certain name; a race; a family.
A unique identifier, generally a string of characters.
An investor in Lloyds of London bearing unlimited liability.
Authority.
definitions.example: Halt in the name of the law!

definitions.definition: To make someone or something incapable of doing something; to disable.
definitions.example: The police officer was incapacitated by a blow to the head.

definitions.definition: To make someone ineligible; to disqualify.
Accompanied by tears; crying, or about to cry.
definitions.example: Aaron was a little tearful until Laura came to reassure him.

definitions.definition: Sorrowful.
A game (originally Chinese) for four players, using a collection of tiles divided into five or six suits.
A solitaire game using the same tiles, where the player wins by removing pairs of matching exposed tiles until none remain.
(heading) Anything that rolls.
A long wide bandage used in surgery.
A large, wide, curling wave that falls back on itself as it breaks on a coast.
(heading) A bird.
A police patrol car or patrolman (rather than an unmarked police car or a detective)
A padded surcingle that is used on horses for training and vaulting.
A roll of titles or (especially) credits played over film or video; television or film credits.
A wheelchair user.
To roller skate.
A protective coating or covering.
To attempt to counter the actions or effects of.
To withstand the actions of.
To oppose.
To be distasteful to.
Below or underneath.
Below.
In a position that is lower in rank, dignity, etc.
Covered up or concealed by something.
To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.
To make (concepts) agree.
To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
definitions.example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.

definitions.definition: To compare in relative force, importance, value, etc.; to estimate.
(dancing) To move toward, and then back from, reciprocally.
definitions.example: to balance partners

definitions.definition: To contract, as a sail, into a narrower compass.
definitions.example: to balance the boom mainsail

definitions.definition: To make the credits and debits of (an account) correspond.
definitions.example: This final payment, or credit, balances the account.

definitions.definition: To be in equilibrium.
To have matching credits and debits.
To weigh in a balance.
To hesitate or fluctuate.
An action wherein someone balances or something is balanced
To be in battle or in a rivalry with another for the same thing, position, or reward; to contend
To be in a position in which it is possible to win or triumph.
To take part in a contest, game or similar event
A point or channel of drainage or off-flow; offlet.
The removal of oil (or an industrial chemical) from a storage facility.
An act of taking off; specifically, the taking away or purchase of goods.
Something taken off; a deduction.
To take off; take away.
One of the casks stowed in the wings of a vessel's hold, being smaller than such as are stowed more amidships.
definitions.example: We hauled out a winger of grog.

definitions.definition: An offensive player who plays on either side of the center.
definitions.example: The center passed to the left winger, who shot and scored.

definitions.definition: The way a person holds and positions their body.
A situation or condition.
One's attitude or the social or political position one takes towards an issue or another person.
The position of someone or something relative to another; position; situation.
To put one's body into a posture or series of postures, especially hoping that one will be noticed and admired
definitions.example: If you're finished posturing in front of the mirror, can I use the bathroom now?

definitions.definition: To pretend to have an opinion or a conviction
definitions.example: The politicians couldn't really care less about the issue: they're just posturing for the media.

definitions.definition: To place in a particular position or attitude; to pose.
definitions.example: to posture oneself; to posture a model

definitions.definition: A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.
definitions.example: The nerves can be seen through the skin.

definitions.definition: A neuron.
A vein in a leaf; a grain in wood
definitions.example: Some plants have ornamental value because of their contrasting nerves.

definitions.definition: Courage, boldness.
definitions.example: He hasn't the nerve to tell her he likes her. What a wimp!

definitions.definition: Patience.
definitions.example: The web-team found git-sed is really a time and nerve saver when doing mass changes on your repositories

definitions.definition: Stamina, endurance, fortitude.
Audacity, gall.
definitions.example: He had the nerve to enter my house uninvited.

definitions.definition: (polymer technology) The elastic resistance of raw rubber or other polymers to permanent deformation during processing.
(in the plural) Agitation caused by fear, stress or other negative emotion.
definitions.example: Ellie had a bad case of nerves before the big test.

definitions.definition: Sinew, tendon.
To give courage.
definitions.example: May their example nerve us to face the enemy.

definitions.definition: To give strength; to supply energy or vigour.
definitions.example: The liquor nerved up several of the men after their icy march.

definitions.definition: To hinder, impede, or prevent.
To bring a legal proceeding against a public official.
definitions.example: President Clinton was impeached by the House in November 1999, but since the Senate acquitted him, he was not removed from office.

definitions.definition: To charge with impropriety; to discredit; to call into question.
To demonstrate in court that a testimony under oath contradicts another testimony from the same person, usually one taken during deposition.
